content: In this lecture, Ian Ball describes zero-sum games, which are games that feature a complete conflict of interest, such that one player’s loss equals another player’s gain. Examples include games like checkers or chess.
title: Lecture 7: Zero-Sum Games

video_metadata.video_tags: 14-12-economic-applications-of-game-theory-fall-2025, checkers, chess, conflict of interest, game, utility, zero-sum
video_metadata.youtube_description:
MIT 14.12 Economic Applications of Game Theory, Fall 2025 
Instructor: Ian Ball 
View the complete course: https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/14-12-economic-applications-of-game-theory-fall-2025/
YouTube Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLUl4u3cNGP63quuKvMHCt3cmTmt0O2qpv

In this lecture, Ian Ball describes zero-sum games, which are games that feature a complete conflict of interest. One player's loss equals another player's gain. Examples includes games like checkers or chess.
